title: A Wretched Hive
summary: The heroes travel to Takodana to infiltrate Varga Besadii Drokko's fortress, posing as entertainment to steal the encryption keys to Varth's fortune. Over several days in the Hutt's court, they navigate gladiatorial spectacle, slave traders, an ISB double agent, and a nighttime assassination attempt — only to discover a captive Jedi Master in the dungeons. The escape costs lives: Demos dies fighting, Varga flees to Bespin, and Varth executes the man the heroes just rescued.

gearNotes.reason: Varth coaches the heroes over encrypted comms from outside Varga's palace. Open comlinks in a Hutt fortress means blown cover. Hard permission gate for the entire court sequence.
The entire mission objective is slicing Varga's datapad to extract the encryption keys. Without a proper slicing interface, the core technical challenge is stepped down or impossible.
High-security detention block locks where Jedi Master Denia is held. Without one, bypassing these suffers heavy penalties.
The Tech's audition — ordered to fix Varga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head. No toolkit means stepped-down rolls on a pass/fail moment.
Multi-scene combat from the hit squad through the fortress escape. No medical facilities available.
Underworld social currency in Blackwind Point. Opens doors in criminal circles where credits draw suspicion. Varth's shopping list should include this.
Plant bugs near the Iron Ring delegation or Lady Sinde's private meetings. The intel from these events reveals Project Leviathan's slave labor connection.
Intercepting unencrypted palace communications — security patrol schedules, delivery timings, who talks to whom.
Recording evidence of slave trade and Imperial deals. Leverage material for later adventures.
Heavy combat droid at the filtration plant and elite security droids in the dungeon. Ion damage is the purpose-built counter.
Sealed blast doors during fortress lockdown. Overload Mode cuts through any door as a single action.
Covering movement through hostile corridors during lockdown and the hangar escape.
Quick terminal bypass in the dungeon to access stasis controls. Consumable, single action.
Hangar bay area denial against clustered Stormtroopers.
Multi-scene fighting with no rest. Someone is running on fumes by the hangar escape.

scenes.title: Blackwind Point
scenes.readAloud:
You disembark at Blackwind Point — a muddy, desperate shantytown on the far shore of Nymeve Lake. The air is thick with the smell of rotting vegetation and desperation. Refugees huddle in makeshift shelters while criminals eye your gear with open hunger.

The growl of repulsor-bikes cuts through the noise. A swoop gang circles your landing pad, engines revving. Their leader — a scarred human woman with a vibro-blade strapped to her leg — pulls up and kills her engine.

"Welcome to the other side of the lake. Landing tax is five hundred credits. Non-negotiable."
scenes.gmNotes:
This scene covers the heroes' entire arrival arc on Takodana: the Kessra shakedown, the investigation to find Raden, the meeting at the filtration plant, and the Trandoshan hit squad attack.

KESSRA CONSEQUENCE GATE: How the heroes handle Kessra determines a conditional event inside the fortress later. If they humiliate her (fight and win decisively, or intimidate her publicly), Kessra takes it personally. She reappears inside Varga's fortress during the court phase (Scene 3) as a hired security contractor and springs an aviary ambush targeting specific heroes by name. If they pay or handle it diplomatically, Kessra does NOT reappear — no grudge, no aviary scene.

INVESTIGATION: Varth has briefed the heroes — they need to find Warrick Raden, a Devaronian fixer who can get them into Varga's court. Raden has gone to ground at the old Water Filtration Plant on the edge of the swamp. Social investigation through the encampment locates him.

RADEN: A nervous wreck hiding in a maintenance shed. He thought Varth was dead. He confirms he can get the heroes inside as 'entertainment' — gladiators, gamblers, or mercenaries. He warns that Varga has become paranoid and obsessed with his new Imperial guests.

HIT SQUAD: Varga sent Trandoshan mercenaries and a heavy combat droid to silence Raden. The fight takes place in cramped machinery — steam pipes burst, obscuring vision. The heroes MUST protect Raden — if he dies, their easy route into the palace dies with him.

AFTER THE FIGHT: Raden proposes presenting the heroes as entertainment. The skiff ride across Nymeve Lake to Varga's fortress is atmospheric: a moss-covered stone ruin retrofitted with gaudy tech and heavy weapons looming through the mist.

npcs.name: Kessra
npcs.notes: Scarred human woman. Leader of The Vipers. Consequence gate — may reappear in fortress if humiliated here.
npcs.behaviour: Demands 500 credits landing tax. Tests the heroes' nerve. If humiliated, holds a personal grudge that triggers the aviary ambush in Scene 3.
dialogue: "Landing tax is five hundred credits. Non-negotiable."
"You think you're tough? Everyone who comes here thinks they're tough. Most of them are wrong."
"I remember faces. Especially the ones that embarrass me."

passive.name: Fleet Footed
passive.description: May take 2 maneuvers per turn instead of 1.

actions.name: Vibroblade Slash
actions.description: Melee attack with vibroblade. Fast and vicious.

actions.name: Drive-By Shot
actions.description: Ranged attack from a moving swoop. +1 zone movement after.

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Move without provoking reactions.

exploit.name: Hit and Fade
exploit.description: After attacking, immediately mount swoop and move 2 zones.

threatBuild.notes: Kessra is a recurring NPC. Consequence gate: humiliation here triggers aviary ambush in Scene 3.

npcs.name: The Vipers
npcs.notes: Armed with blasters and vibro-blades. Intimidation over combat skill.

passive.name: Fleet Footed
passive.description: May take 2 maneuvers per turn.

actions.name: Blaster Snap
actions.description: Ranged attack.

actions.name: Vibroblade Lunge
actions.description: Melee attack.

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Move without provoking reactions.

exploit.name: Scatter
exploit.description: If leader is downed, all Vipers disengage and flee.

threatBuild.notes: Minion gang. Masterful+ = takedown. Paper tigers — flee if Kessra falls.

npcs.name: Warrick Raden
npcs.notes: Nervous wreck. Owes Varth a debt. The heroes' entry ticket to Varga's palace.
npcs.behaviour: Terrified, armed with a blaster, jumping at shadows. Relieved when he hears Varth is alive. Agrees to help if protected.
dialogue: "Varth? He's alive? Stars help me — he's alive."
"I know how to get you in. Varga is hosting a delegation of high rollers and business partners. He wants entertainment — gladiators, gamblers, mercenaries."
"Once we're inside... you're on your own."
npcs.intel: Raden was a middleman in Varga's network — skimmed credits after Varth was captured. Got caught. Has hand-drawn maps of the fortress, guard shift times, and Varga's paranoid habits.

passive.name: Fixer's Contacts
passive.description: Raden knows everyone in Varga's operation. Can identify guards, routines, and weak points.

actions.name: Spill Info
actions.description: Provide palace intel when motivated.

actions.name: Panic Shot
actions.description: Wild blaster shot when cornered. Low accuracy.

maneuver.name: Bolt
maneuver.description: Attempt to flee. Must be intercepted with Athletics or Evasion.

threatBuild.notes: Raden is not a fighter. His value is information, not combat.

npcs.name: Trandoshan Mercenaries
npcs.notes: Varga's hit squad sent to silence Raden. Using the industrial environment to hunt.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore the first [Weakened] condition per scene.

actions.name: Claw Slash
actions.description: Melee attack with natural claws. +1 Power.

actions.name: Hunting Rifle
actions.description: Ranged attack at medium range.

maneuver.name: Scent Track
maneuver.description: In obscured conditions, locate one hidden enemy. Ignores concealment from steam.

exploit.name: Regeneration
exploit.description: At end of turn, heal 1 Vitality. Trandoshan biology.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Brutes. Tough = ignore Fleeting damage. Scent Track bypasses steam concealment. A real fight.

npcs.name: Heavy Combat Droid
npcs.notes: Blocking the main entrance of the filtration plant. Heavy armor and weapons.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore the first [Weakened] condition per scene.

actions.name: Heavy Blaster Cannon
actions.description: Ranged attack with integrated heavy blaster. +1 Power.

actions.name: Battering Ram
actions.description: Charge + melee. Destroys cover.

maneuver.name: Advance
maneuver.description: Move forward 1 zone. Ignores difficult terrain.

gambit.name: Suppressive Barrage
gambit.description: Area fire. All targets in zone gain [Suppressed].

exploit.name: Overload
exploit.description: When reduced to 0 Vitality, explodes. [Hazard 3] in zone.

threatBuild.notes: Elite Brute Droid. Defense 8. Iron Clad: uses Defense vs Evasion attacks. Vulnerable to Ion damage.

scenes.hazards: Muddy terrain at the landing pad. Cramped industrial machinery at the filtration plant. Bursting steam pipes. Collapsing catwalks.

decisions.consequence: Marked as easy targets. Pickpockets and grifters will target you. No aviary ambush later.
Local respect earned. But Kessra holds a grudge — she reappears inside the fortress for the aviary ambush (Scene 3).

decisions.choice: Calm Raden and negotiate his help
decisions.consequence: He cooperates willingly and provides detailed fortress intel.
He cooperates but looks for ways to sell the heroes out at the first opportunity.

narrativeLinks.note: Kessra's humiliation here triggers the aviary ambush in Scene 3.

skillChecks.context: Staring down Kessra and the Vipers to avoid paying landing tax.
skillChecks.success: Kessra reads you as dangerous. Backs off with a sneer. Locals take notice.
skillChecks.failure: Kessra doesn't flinch. The Vipers close in.

skillChecks.context: Gathering intel on Raden's location from the encampment.
skillChecks.success: Multiple sources confirm: Water Filtration Plant, edge of the swamp. He's been hiding there 3 days.
skillChecks.failure: Conflicting information. Two locations — one is a trap.

skillChecks.context: Calming Raden down. He's panicking with a blaster aimed at you.
skillChecks.success: Raden lowers the blaster. Starts talking. Relieved that Varth is alive.
skillChecks.failure: Raden stays panicky. Blaster stays up. Additional checks needed.

skillChecks.context: Reading the hit squad's approach pattern at the filtration plant.
skillChecks.success: They're coming from three directions — rear maintenance hatch is unguarded.
skillChecks.failure: No clear picture. React to contact.

skillChecks.context: Overloading the filtration machinery for area denial against the hit squad.
skillChecks.success: Steam erupts in a wall of scalding vapor. Trandoshans are forced back 2 rounds.
skillChecks.failure: Machinery sputters. Partial obstruction only.

skillChecks.context: Building a convincing cover identity for the palace infiltration.
skillChecks.success: Air-tight cover. Raden vouches convincingly.
skillChecks.failure: Cover has gaps. Varga's people may ask uncomfortable questions.

encounters.name: Viper Shakedown
encounters.description: Kessra and 6 Vipers demand 500 credits. Social challenge with combat fallback.
encounters.tactics: Kessra leads verbally. Vipers fan out to cut off exits. If fighting starts, they use swoops for hit-and-run.

encounters.name: Trandoshan Hit Squad
encounters.trigger: After meeting Raden at the filtration plant
encounters.description: 4 Trandoshan Mercenaries breach from multiple entry points. Heavy Combat Droid blocks the main exit. Protect Raden.
encounters.tactics: Trandoshans use scent-tracking in the steam. The droid advances slowly, absorbing fire.

environmentMechanics.name: Muddy Landing Pad
environmentMechanics.effect: Difficult terrain. Movement costs double. Swoops ignore this.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay on the ferrocrete pad edges.

environmentMechanics.name: Industrial Steam
environmentMechanics.trigger: Burst pipes or deliberate overload at filtration plant
environmentMechanics.effect: Scalding steam fills a zone. [Hazard 1] per round. Concealment.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Breath mask reduces to no damage. Stay low.

environmentMechanics.name: Cramped Machinery
environmentMechanics.effect: Narrow corridors between machinery. No flanking. Limits movement to single-file.

items.name: Palace Maps (sketch)
items.note: Raden's hand-drawn maps of Varga's fortress. Rough but useful.

items.name: Trandoshan Weapons
items.note: Scavenged medium blasters and a hunting rifle.

rewards.narrative: Raden survives. Hit squad eliminated. Cover identities established. Entry to Varga's court secured.

pacing.openingBeat: The Vipers circle the landing pad.
pacing.risingAction: Investigation through the encampment. Raden found at the filtration plant. Hit squad attacks.
pacing.climax: The heavy combat droid advances. Must be dealt with to escape.
pacing.resolution: The skiff ride across Nymeve Lake. Varga's fortress looms through the mist.

scenes.title: The Court of Varga
scenes.subtitle: The Throne Room
scenes.readAloud:
The skiff glides across Nymeve Lake through hanging curtains of mist. The fortress materializes ahead — a moss-covered stone ruin retrofitted with gaudy tech, blaster turrets, and gaudy banners bearing Varga's sigil. You dock at a landing platform ringed by Gamorrean guards.

Raden leads you through damp, torch-lit corridors that smell of mold and roasting meat. You emerge into Varga's Throne Room — a sensory assault of thick smoke, frantic jizz-wailer music, and a crowd of sycophants, thieves, and scavengers.

At the far end, resting on a massive reinforced hover-sled, sits Varga the Hutt. He is bloated, scarred, and flanked by a polished silver protocol droid. Varga barks in Huttese, his deep voice shaking the room. The droid translates:

"The Mighty Varga is bored with beggars. Prove you are worth his air."
scenes.gmNotes:
Varga demands an immediate demonstration. Each hero must prove their value:
- The Muscle gets a fight against a Gamorrean in the pit.
- The Face gets a battle of wits with courtiers.
- The Tech is ordered to fix Varga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head.
- Other specialists demonstrate as appropriate.

If heroes succeed, Varga welcomes them as 'guests' (prisoners with privileges). He then turns on Raden — recognizing him. Varga bellows in Huttese. Gamorreans drag Raden to the dungeons as punishment. Raden's screams serve as a warning.

TC-663 INTRODUCTION: During the audition, the silver protocol droid (TC-663 / 'Six-Six') translates for Varga. If the heroes use Switch's code phrase from Adventure 1, TC-663's photoreceptors flash recognition. He begins covertly assisting them — providing palace maps, guard rotations, and the location of Varga's encrypted datapad. Without the code phrase, TC-663 remains cautious and provides only partial assistance.

NARRATIVE FUNCTION: This scene establishes the heroes inside the fortress, introduces the key NPCs they'll interact with during the court phase, and rips away their guide (Raden) to raise the stakes.

npcs.name: Varga the Hutt
npcs.notes: Bloated, scarred, paranoid. Sits on an electrum-plated hover-sled. Never speaks Basic — uses TC-663. Full name: Varga Besadii Drokko.
npcs.behaviour: Demands entertainment. Bored, jaded, suspicious. Values spectacle over competence. Turns on Raden the moment he recognizes him.
dialogue: "The Mighty Varga is bored with beggars. Prove you are worth his air." (via TC-663)
"This one... this one I KNOW." (pointing at Raden, via TC-663)
"Take the thief to the dungeons. Let him remember what it costs to steal from Besadii." (via TC-663)

passive.name: Absolute Authority
passive.description: All allies in Varga's court gain +1 to Resist social attacks. His presence is law.

actions.name: Command
actions.description: Order one ally to immediately attack or take an action.

actions.name: Crushing Tail
actions.description: Melee attack at engaged range. +2 Power. Hutt mass.

maneuver.name: Hover-Sled
maneuver.description: Move on repulsor sled. Not impeded by terrain.

gambit.name: Example
gambit.description: Order guards to execute a prisoner or destroy property. All witnesses gain [Shaken].

exploit.name: Bounty
exploit.description: Declare a bounty on one target. All mercenaries in the fortress gain +1 Power vs that target for the scene.

threatBuild.notes: Varga is a crime lord, not a fighter. Boss x2 Vitality = 24. He fights through his army. Direct combat is a mistake.

npcs.name: TC-663 ('Six-Six')
npcs.notes: Varga's translator. Secretly an agent of Switch from Adventure 1.
npcs.behaviour: Translates for Varga with exaggerated courtesy. If heroes use Switch's code phrase, he provides covert assistance. Without it, he remains cautious.
dialogue: "The Mighty Varga welcomes you to his humble court." (subtle emphasis on 'humble' — TC-663's dry humor)
"If I may suggest... the eastern corridor is less frequently patrolled after the third watch." (whispered, if turned)
"Master Switch sends his regards." (if code phrase used)

passive.name: Double Agent
passive.description: TC-663 serves Switch. If heroes use Switch's code phrase, he provides palace maps, datapad location, and guard rotations.

actions.name: Translate
actions.description: Translate Huttese. Can subtly alter translations to help or hinder.

actions.name: Whispered Intel
actions.description: Pass information discreetly during court proceedings.

maneuver.name: Blend In
maneuver.description: As a protocol droid, TC-663 is invisible to court security. Can move freely.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant spy. His real value is turning him into an asset using Switch's code phrase from Adventure 1.

npcs.name: Igren Demos
npcs.notes: Varga's right hand. Secretly Force-sensitive and plotting a coup. Watches the heroes with suspicion.
npcs.behaviour: Officious and ambitious. Watches the heroes during the audition and takes mental notes. If the deception check fails, he is ordered to watch them closely.

passive.name: Untrained Sensitivity
passive.description: Demos can sense Force disturbances but cannot control it. He senses Denia's awakening and any Force use in the palace.

actions.name: Force Slam
actions.description: Crude Force push. Targets Endure. Knocks target back 1 zone.

actions.name: Vibro-Staff Strike
actions.description: Melee attack with vibro-staff.

maneuver.name: Force Pull
maneuver.description: Yank one object or person 1 zone closer.

exploit.name: Desperate Surge
exploit.description: Once per encounter, double Power on a Force attack. Costs 2 Vitality.

threatBuild.notes: Elite mini-boss. Untrained Force user — dangerous but sloppy. Plotting to usurp Varga. Full combat in Scene 5.

npcs.name: Gamorrean Guards
npcs.notes: Vibro-axe wielding palace guards.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore first [Weakened] per scene.

actions.name: Vibro-Axe Chop
actions.description: Melee attack. +1 Power.

actions.name: Headbutt
actions.description: Close range. Target must Endure or gain [Stunned].

maneuver.name: Intimidating Advance
maneuver.description: Move + target resists or gains [Shaken].

exploit.name: Berserk
exploit.description: When below half Vitality, +1 Power on all attacks.

threatBuild.notes: Minion Brutes. Tough. Masterful+ = takedown. Palace guards — vibro-axes and bad attitudes.

scenes.hazards: Hostile court environment. Surrounded by Varga's forces. No weapons allowed in the throne room (confiscated at the door). Violence = instant death.

decisions.choice: Impress Varga with a spectacular audition
decisions.consequence: Earn 'guest' status and freedom to move within approved areas of the palace.

decisions.choice: Try to intervene when Raden is taken
decisions.consequence: Extremely dangerous — would blow cover immediately. Varga's court is heavily armed.

skillChecks.context: The Muscle's audition — fighting a Gamorrean in Varga's pit.
skillChecks.success: Decisive victory. Varga is impressed. [Guest] status earned.
skillChecks.failure: A sloppy showing. Varga is unimpressed. Cover is thinner.

skillChecks.context: The Face's audition — a battle of wits with Varga's courtiers.
skillChecks.success: Varga laughs. He likes you. The court takes notice.
skillChecks.failure: Flat performance. Varga yawns. Cover survives but barely.

skillChecks.context: The Tech's audition — fixing Varga's hover-sled with a blaster at your head.
skillChecks.success: Sled purrs like new. Varga is delighted. Gives you run of the workshop.
skillChecks.failure: Sled sputters. Not broken worse, but Varga is skeptical.

skillChecks.context: Maintaining cover identity under Varga's scrutiny.
skillChecks.success: Varga buys it completely. No further questions.
skillChecks.failure: Varga is suspicious. Igren Demos is ordered to watch you closely.

skillChecks.context: Reading the court dynamics — who has power, who is plotting.
skillChecks.success: TC-663 keeps glancing at you meaningfully. Igren Demos watches Varga like a predator.
skillChecks.failure: Overwhelming sensory chaos. Too many players to read.

encounters.name: The Audition
encounters.description: Each PC must prove their worth to Varga in their specialty. Muscle fights, Face talks, Tech fixes.
encounters.tactics: Varga is bored and jaded. Only genuine skill or entertainment impresses him.

environmentMechanics.name: Hostile Court
environmentMechanics.effect: Surrounded by Varga's forces. No weapons allowed in the throne room. Violence = instant death.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Play the part. Do not break cover.

environmentMechanics.name: Varga's Attention Span
environmentMechanics.effect: Varga loses interest quickly. Failed auditions don't cause immediate expulsion but reduce trust.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Make it entertaining. Varga values spectacle over competence.

rewards.narrative: 'Guest' status in Varga's court — freedom to move within approved areas. Raden is in the dungeons.

pacing.openingBeat: The skiff ride across the misty lake. The fortress looms.
pacing.risingAction: Each PC faces their audition under Varga's bored gaze.
pacing.climax: Varga's judgment. Then Raden is recognized and dragged to the dungeon.
pacing.resolution: The heroes are 'welcomed' to court. TC-663 may become an ally. The clock starts.

scenes.title: Currying Favor
scenes.subtitle: The Multi-Day Court Sandbox
scenes.readAloud:
Over the next several days, you navigate the dangerous politics of Varga's court. Admiral Varth coaches you over encrypted comms, pointing out targets and reminding you to keep your eyes on the prize.

The court operates on Varga's whims. Events arrive unannounced — delegations, spectacles, celebrations, punishments. Between them, the heroes have windows to explore, make contacts, and pursue the mission. Every day brings new opportunities and new dangers.
scenes.gmNotes:
This is a MULTI-DAY SANDBOX scene. The court phase plays out over 3-5 in-game days with structured events and freeform exploration between them. The GM runs events in order, with 'between' activities available during downtime.

STRUCTURED COURT EVENTS (run in order):

1. THE GLADIATOR ARENA (Day 1): Varga announces a gladiator tournament to 'test' his new guests. The heroes can fight directly (Shockboxer/Duelist spotlight) or sponsor a fighter. The arena is a sunken pit in the fortress courtyard. Winning builds reputation and earns Varga's favor. The reigning champion is Gorrak — a scarred Trandoshan veteran. Defeating him earns significant court standing.

2. THE IRON RING DELEGATION (Day 2): A Zabrak slaver named Vel Crekk leads the Iron Ring delegation into court with chained prisoners — Twi'leks, Rodians, and one massive Wookiee warrior (Grakkus). During the negotiations, a Twi'lek slave serving Varga's drinks attempts to poison the Hutt's goblet. If the heroes notice (Insight check) and intervene — either by stopping the poison or by covering for the slave — they can earn instant favor with Varga (saving his life) or with the slave (protecting him). The slave's name is Narek. The Wookiee warrior (Grakkus) is sold to Varga as arena muscle but remains unchained — he fights willingly because the Iron Ring holds his family. Grakkus stays in the fortress after the slavers leave. He is a SLEEPER THREAT who participates in the Knife in the Dark event.

3. LADY SINDE'S IMPERIAL DELEGATION (Day 3-4, final event): Lady Sinde arrives with a 4-Stormtrooper escort. She is ostensibly an Imperial logistics officer negotiating Tibanna gas shipments for Project Leviathan. Her real identity: Victra Sinde, ISB (Imperial Security Bureau) double agent embedded in the Leviathan logistics chain. She monitors Imperial-Hutt collusion for the ISB while maintaining her logistics cover. Heroes may recognize her from Organa's intelligence network or discover her ISB identity via Investigation or Stealth (eavesdropping on her encrypted comms). Sinde despises Varga — he's a tool. The Empire is already planning to discard him once Leviathan is complete.

BETWEEN-EVENT ACTIVITIES (available during downtime, any order):

• GOME THE MONEYCHANGER: An elderly Duros moneychanger who handles Varga's financial transactions. Rambling, morally indifferent, obsessed with numbers. If engaged in conversation about finances (Charm or Investigation), he reveals that Varga has departure contingency plans — a fallback headquarters on Bespin, in Cloud City. This is the critical intel that connects Adventure 2 to Adventure 3. Gome doesn't understand the significance of what he's sharing; he just likes talking about money.

• TC-663 NIGHTTIME VISIT: Between events, TC-663 visits the heroes' quarters at night. If they've activated him with Switch's code phrase, he delivers Demos's encrypted comm traffic — intercepted messages between Demos and an Imperial contact. The messages reference 'the prisoner' (Denia) and Demos's plan to sell her to the Inquisitorius. This is the first concrete evidence that someone important is hidden in the dungeons.

• KESSRA'S AVIARY AMBUSH (CONDITIONAL — only if heroes humiliated Kessra in Scene 1): Kessra has been hired as a security contractor inside Varga's fortress. She sets a personal vendetta trap — targeting 1-2 heroes by name (the ones who humiliated her). She lures them to the fortress aviary (a domed enclosure housing Takodana predatory raptors) under a false pretense (a message from 'a contact'). Once inside, she locks the doors and releases the raptors. This is NOT Varga's doing — it's personal. Kessra wants revenge, not to blow the heroes' cover. If the heroes kill the raptors and confront Kessra, she can be intimidated into backing down permanently or killed quietly. If they escape, she tries again later.

• KNIFE IN THE DARK (Night of Day 3 or 4): A nighttime assassination attempt. The Wookiee sleeper Grakkus teams with Narek the Twi'lek (if saved from the poison attempt — he switches sides out of gratitude to whoever saved him, but his gratitude makes him manipulable by the real organizer) and a Quarren assassin named Skreev. Skreev is the organizer — an agent sent by one of Varga's rivals to eliminate the heroes (who are seen as Varga's new favorites). They strike at night in the heroes' quarters. Grakkus provides muscle. The heroes must fight in close quarters, possibly in the dark, with limited weapons (their good gear may be locked away). This is a dangerous encounter — Grakkus alone is formidable.

npcs.name: Gorrak
npcs.notes: Scarred Trandoshan arena veteran. Reigning champion of Varga's pit. Fights with a vibro-axe and shield.

passive.name: Arena Veteran
passive.description: Gorrak has fought hundreds of bouts. He reads feints and recovers from mistakes. Immune to [Disoriented].

actions.name: Vibro-Axe Slam
actions.description: Heavy overhead melee attack. +1 Power.

actions.name: Shield Bash
actions.description: Melee bash that knocks back 1 zone.

maneuver.name: Raise Shield
maneuver.description: Gain [Cover 1] until next turn.

exploit.name: Regeneration
exploit.description: Heal 1 Vitality at end of turn. Trandoshan biology.

threatBuild.notes: Elite Brute gladiator. Tougher than standard Trandoshans (elite = 1.5x Vitality). Crowd favorite — Varga bets heavily on him.

npcs.name: Iron Ring Slavers
npcs.notes: Led by Vel Crekk (Zabrak). Arguing about 'damaged merchandise' (dying slaves).

passive.name: Implied Violence
passive.description: Social attacks inflict [Shaken] instead of normal social conditions.

actions.name: Slaver's Lash
actions.description: Melee attack with shock-whip. Targets Endure.

actions.name: Threaten
actions.description: Social attack using implied force. Targets Resist.

maneuver.name: Loom
maneuver.description: Move to close range. Target resists or gains [Rattled].

gambit.name: Crack the Whip
gambit.description: Area social attack. All witnesses gain [Shaken].

exploit.name: Chain Gang
exploit.description: If a target is [Prone], automatically apply [Restrained].

threatBuild.notes: Enforcer slavers. Fearless: immune to [Shaken]/[Rattled]. Loathsome but dangerous.

npcs.name: Narek
npcs.notes: Serving slave who attempts to poison Varga's drink during the Iron Ring event. If saved by the heroes, becomes a potential informant — but also participates in the Knife in the Dark (manipulated by Skreev).

passive.name: Invisible Servant
passive.description: Narek moves through the court unseen. Slaves are furniture to the powerful.

actions.name: Concealed Blade
actions.description: Small hidden knife. Surprise attack.

actions.name: Poison Application
actions.description: Apply toxin to food or drink. Targets Endure if consumed.

maneuver.name: Blend Into Crowd
maneuver.description: Disappear into the servant population. Cannot be targeted until located.

threatBuild.notes: Narek is desperate, not evil. He wants to poison Varga because the Hutt sold his family. If saved, he is grateful but emotionally broken — Skreev exploits this.

npcs.name: Grakkus
npcs.notes: Sold by the Iron Ring as arena muscle. Fights willingly because the slavers hold his family. Stays in the fortress as a sleeper threat. Participates in the Knife in the Dark assassination attempt.

passive.name: Wookiee Rage
passive.description: When below half Vitality, +2 Power on melee attacks.

actions.name: Crushing Grip
actions.description: Grapple attack. +1 Power. Target gains [Restrained] on hit.

actions.name: Rending Claws
actions.description: Vicious unarmed melee attack.

maneuver.name: Charge
maneuver.description: Move 1 zone and engage. Target resists or gains [Rattled].

gambit.name: Hurl
gambit.description: Throw a [Restrained] target 1 zone. They gain [Prone].

exploit.name: Last Stand
exploit.description: When reduced to 0 Vitality, make one final attack at +3 Power before going down.

threatBuild.notes: Dangerous melee combatant. Heavy chassis = significant damage. The real muscle in the Knife in the Dark encounter.

npcs.name: Skreev
npcs.notes: Agent sent by one of Varga's rivals. Organizes the Knife in the Dark assassination attempt. Manipulates Narek and coordinates with Grakkus.

passive.name: Ambush Predator
passive.description: +2 Power on the first attack if target is unaware or surprised.

actions.name: Vibrodagger Strike
actions.description: Fast melee attack with a concealed vibrodagger.

actions.name: Thrown Blade
actions.description: Ranged thrown weapon at short range.

maneuver.name: Fade
maneuver.description: Disengage and gain [Concealed] if in dim light or shadow.

exploit.name: Escape Artist
exploit.description: If reduced below half Vitality, Skreev attempts to flee. Athletics or Evasion to catch.

threatBuild.notes: Assassin. Ambush Predator makes his opening strike dangerous. Flee instinct if outmatched.

npcs.name: Takodana Raptors
npcs.notes: Released by Kessra during the aviary ambush. Fast, vicious, territorial.

passive.name: Swooping Attack
passive.description: Raptors attack from above. Targets cannot use cover against their attacks.

actions.name: Talon Rake
actions.description: Diving melee attack with talons.

actions.name: Beak Strike
actions.description: Pecking attack at eyes/face. Causes [Disoriented] on hit.

maneuver.name: Circle
maneuver.description: Fly out of melee range. Only ranged attacks can reach.

threatBuild.notes: Minion beasts. Masterful+ = takedown. Dangerous in enclosed spaces because cover is negated. 4 raptors in a locked aviary = chaotic.

npcs.name: Lady Sinde
npcs.type: Imperial Logistics Officer / ISB Double Agent
npcs.notes: Real name: Victra Sinde. ISB agent posing as Leviathan logistics officer. Arrives with 4 Stormtroopers. Monitors Imperial-Hutt collusion for the ISB while negotiating Tibanna gas shipments.

passive.name: Imperial Authority
passive.description: Lady Sinde commands Stormtroopers. Her orders are obeyed without question.

actions.name: Direct Fire
actions.description: Order a Stormtrooper to attack immediately.

actions.name: Cutting Assessment
actions.description: Social attack that dismantles cover stories. Targets Resist.

maneuver.name: Issue Orders
maneuver.description: One ally gains a bonus maneuver.

gambit.name: Ultimatum
gambit.description: Target must comply or be marked for elimination.

exploit.name: Call Reinforcements
exploit.description: Summon 4 Stormtrooper minions to her location.

threatBuild.notes: Commander. Resist 5. She is a logistics officer, not a frontline fighter. Her danger is the army behind her. ISB identity is a discoverable secondary challenge.

npcs.name: Gome
npcs.notes: Elderly palace moneychanger. Rambling, morally indifferent, obsessed with numbers. Reveals Bespin/Cloud City as Varga's fallback headquarters.
npcs.behaviour: Talks endlessly about financial details if asked. Doesn't see the moral dimension of anything. Shares critical Bespin intel without realizing its importance.
dialogue: "The accounts for the secondary operations? Oh, those route through a Bespin address. Cloud City, actually. Very elegant banking infrastructure up there — did you know they use a triple-ledger system?"
"Varga keeps a contingency vault on Cloud City. Standard Hutt practice. If things go wrong here, he takes the Chariot and... well, you understand."
"I don't make moral judgments about money. Money is just numbers. Beautiful, precise numbers."

passive.name: Loves Numbers
passive.description: Gome will talk endlessly about financial details if asked. He doesn't care about crime — only about elegant accounting.

actions.name: Financial Breakdown
actions.description: Explain Varga's shipping operations in excruciating detail.

actions.name: Shrug
actions.description: Deflect moral questions with bureaucratic indifference.

maneuver.name: Pull Records
maneuver.description: Access accounting data from memory.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant. The only NPC who will freely discuss Varga's operations — he just doesn't see the moral dimension. CRITICAL: provides Bespin/Cloud City intel for Adv3.

scenes.hazards: Court politics — any misstep can blow the heroes' cover. Multiple factions with competing agendas. Nighttime assassination attempt.

decisions.choice: Fight in the gladiator arena personally
decisions.consequence: Builds reputation fast. Risk of injury. Gorrak is a tough opponent.

decisions.choice: Save Narek from the poison consequences
decisions.consequence: Earns instant favor if Varga is saved. Narek becomes an informant — but also later participates in Knife in the Dark.
Discovering her dual role provides leverage but risks drawing ISB attention to the heroes.

decisions.choice: Turn TC-663 into a full asset
decisions.consequence: If activated with Switch's code phrase, he provides Demos's encrypted comms — critical dungeon intel.

narrativeLinks.note: Gome's intel about Bespin/Cloud City as Varga's fallback directly sets up Adventure 3.
TC-663's delivery of Demos's encrypted comms leads the heroes to investigate the dungeons.

skillChecks.context: Fighting Gorrak in the gladiator arena.
skillChecks.success: Decisive victory. The crowd roars. Varga is delighted. Significant court reputation earned.
skillChecks.failure: Gorrak wins. The hero survives (arena fights are to submission) but reputation suffers.

skillChecks.context: Noticing Narek's poison attempt during the Iron Ring delegation.
skillChecks.success: You spot Narek's hand trembling as he serves the goblet. Something is in the drink.
skillChecks.failure: Nothing seems unusual. The drink reaches Varga.

skillChecks.context: Maintaining cover throughout the multi-day court phase.
skillChecks.success: Cover holds perfectly throughout the stay.
skillChecks.failure: A suspicious courtier begins asking pointed questions. Demos takes notice.

skillChecks.context: Discovering Lady Sinde's ISB identity through her encrypted comms or behaviour patterns.
skillChecks.success: Her comm frequencies don't match standard logistics channels. ISB encryption protocols. She's a double agent.
skillChecks.failure: She appears to be exactly what she claims — a logistics officer.

skillChecks.context: Eavesdropping on Lady Sinde's private meeting with Varga.
skillChecks.success: Full conversation: next Tibanna shipment coordinates, payment terms, mention of 'the Accountant's records.'
skillChecks.failure: Spotted by a guard. Must talk your way out — Deception check or cover blown.

skillChecks.context: Engaging Gome in conversation about Varga's finances.
skillChecks.success: Gome rambles about contingency accounts, departure plans, Cloud City banking. Critical Bespin intel acquired.
skillChecks.failure: Gome talks about exchange rates for 20 minutes. No actionable intel.

skillChecks.context: Activating TC-663 using Switch's code phrase.
skillChecks.success: TC-663's photoreceptors flash recognition. He provides Demos's encrypted comms referencing 'the prisoner.'
skillChecks.failure: TC-663 is cautious. Provides partial information only.
skillChecks.narrativePacing: Key check. The code phrase from Adv1 reduces this to T0/P0.

skillChecks.context: Slicing Varga's datapad to extract the encryption keys during a window of opportunity.
skillChecks.success: Encryption keys extracted cleanly. No trace left.
skillChecks.failure: Partial extraction. Silent alarm triggers. Lockdown in 5 rounds.

encounters.name: Gladiator Arena
encounters.description: One-on-one arena fight against Gorrak. Crowd watches. Varga bets heavily.
encounters.tactics: Gorrak uses his shield defensively and waits for openings. He's patient and experienced.

encounters.name: Knife in the Dark
encounters.trigger: Night of Day 3-4 — assassination attempt in heroes' quarters
encounters.description: Grakkus (Wookiee), Skreev (Quarren assassin), and potentially Narek (Twi'lek) attack at night. Close quarters, dim light, limited weapons.
encounters.tactics: Skreev enters first through the window, going for the throat. Grakkus smashes through the door. Narek blocks the exit if present. Heroes may be in sleepwear with no armor.

encounters.name: Kessra's Aviary Ambush
encounters.trigger: CONDITIONAL — only if Kessra was humiliated in Scene 1
encounters.description: Kessra lures 1-2 heroes to the aviary and releases 4 Takodana Raptors. Doors are locked.
encounters.tactics: Raptors attack from above. Cover is useless. Must kill the raptors or break through the locked doors.

environmentMechanics.name: Court Surveillance
environmentMechanics.effect: Varga's court has eyes everywhere. Extended absence or unusual behavior is noticed.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Coordinate with TC-663. Use court events as distractions.

environmentMechanics.name: Gladiator Pit
environmentMechanics.effect: Sunken pit. No ranged weapons allowed. Crowd interference — thrown objects, taunts. Varga may change the rules mid-fight.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Pure melee combat. Arena Veteran opponents know the pit.

environmentMechanics.name: Dim Quarters (Knife in the Dark)
environmentMechanics.effect: Darkness. Heroes may be unarmored. Weapons may be locked away. All checks at -1 Power unless light source activated.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Glow Rod eliminates darkness penalty. Keep a weapon under the pillow.

environmentMechanics.name: Locked Aviary
environmentMechanics.effect: Domed enclosure. Doors locked from outside. Raptors negate cover from above.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Kill the raptors. Or Athletics/Tech to break through the locked doors.

items.name: Encryption Keys (partial)
items.note: If datapad slice succeeds. Varth's fortune access codes.

items.name: Bespin Intel
items.note: From Gome: Varga's contingency vault on Cloud City.

items.name: Demos's Encrypted Comms
items.note: From TC-663: references to 'the prisoner' in the dungeons.

rewards.narrative: Intelligence gathered. Reputation built. Assassination attempt survived. The dungeon beckons.

pacing.openingBeat: Days in Varga's court. Three structured events unfold.
pacing.risingAction: Iron Ring slavers. Lady Sinde's delegation. Gome's Bespin intel. TC-663's nighttime delivery.
pacing.climax: Knife in the Dark — assassination attempt at night. Then the dungeon investigation begins.
pacing.resolution: The heroes have intel, reputation, and a lead on the dungeons. Time to move.

scenes.title: Discovering Denia
scenes.subtitle: The Dungeon
scenes.readAloud:
TC-663's intercepted comms mentioned 'the prisoner' — someone hidden in the dungeon that Demos wants to sell to the Inquisitorius. You descend into the restricted lower levels, past sealed blast doors and silent guard droids.

The detention block is cold and humming with machinery. In the deepest cell, a stasis pod is hooked to life-support systems. Through the frost-covered viewport, you see a human woman in tattered Jedi robes, suspended in a Force-induced coma.

Her eyes are closed. But you feel something — a whisper at the edge of your consciousness. Not words. A feeling. Like drowning in slow motion.

In the adjacent cell, Warrick Raden huddles in the corner. Beaten, bruised, but alive. He looks up with hollow eyes.

Admiral Varth's voice comes over the encrypted comlink. Cold. Interested. Not the voice you've been hearing.

"Grab her. She could be useful. She's a Jedi historian... or at least worth a fortune in bounty."
scenes.gmNotes:
This is the campaign's first major pivot. Two discoveries in one scene:

1. JEDI MASTER DENIA: Found in stasis since shortly after Order 66. Kept by Varga as a trophy to auction to the Inquisitorius. She's weak, barely alive, and telepathically reaching out. Rescuing her is MANDATORY per campaign continuity rules — Varth insists because he recognizes her value as a Force-sensitive key and Jedi historian. But the way he insists is the first red flag: 'Grab her. She could be useful.' He treats her as an asset, not a person.

2. WARRICK RADEN: Found beaten in the adjacent cell. The heroes rescued him from the hit squad, he got them inside, and Varga threw him in the dungeon. He's alive and can walk, but he's broken. The heroes will rescue him here — and Varth will execute him at the hangar in Scene 6.

EAVESDROP ON DEMOS/DRACO HOLO-CALL: While in the dungeon area, the heroes can overhear (or TC-663 alerts them to) a holo-call between Igren Demos and Inquisitor Draco. Demos is reporting that the prisoner is still viable. Draco's response: 'I am on my way, with the Assiduous.' This starts the DEATH CLOCK — Draco's Star Destroyer is inbound. The heroes must escape before it arrives.

VARTH'S RED FLAG #1: His voice changes when he realizes Denia is a Jedi. He is interested, calculating. He immediately pivots from 'leave her' to 'grab her' — but his reasoning is entirely utilitarian. 'She's a Jedi historian... or at least worth a fortune in bounty.' This is the first crack in the charming employer facade. Players should feel uncomfortable with how quickly he commodified a helpless person.

npcs.name: Jedi Master Denia
npcs.notes: In stasis for ~2 years since Order 66. Powerful but frail. Doesn't know the Empire exists yet.

passive.name: Weakened Master
passive.description: Denia's stats represent her full potential. Currently: all physical actions [Weakened], Vitality halved (4), single action per round. Recovers over Adventures 4-9.

actions.name: Force Barrier
actions.description: Create a telekinetic shield. Allies in zone gain [Cover 2] for 1 round.

actions.name: Telepathic Bond
actions.description: Communicate silently with one target. Share images, emotions, warnings.

maneuver.name: Sense Danger
maneuver.description: Detect hostile intent within the scene. Provides tactical warning.

exploit.name: Desperate Burst
exploit.description: Once per adventure, unleash full Force power. All enemies in zone gain [Stunned]. Denia collapses afterward.

threatBuild.notes: Jedi Master at full power — but weakened by 2 years in stasis. Current effective stats are halved. She is an NPC ally, not under player control.

npcs.name: Elite Guard Droids
npcs.notes: Guarding the high-security detention block.

passive.name: Disciplined Fire
passive.description: +1 Power on weapon attacks.

actions.name: Integrated Blaster
actions.description: Built-in medium blaster. Cannot be disarmed.

actions.name: Stun Shock
actions.description: Melee. Target must Endure or gain [Stunned].

maneuver.name: Lock Down
maneuver.description: Seal one door or access point in the zone.

gambit.name: Overwatch Protocol
gambit.description: Hold action. Fire on first movement in line of sight.

exploit.name: Alert Network
exploit.description: Trigger palace-wide alarm. All security responds.

threatBuild.notes: Iron Clad security droids. Defense 6. Vulnerable to Ion damage — Ion Grenade drops them instantly.

npcs.name: Warrick Raden
npcs.notes: Found beaten in the adjacent cell. Alive but broken. (Recurring — same stat block as Scene 1.)

scenes.hazards: High-security zone. Silent alarms if stasis machinery is tampered with. Igren Demos can sense Force disturbances. Draco inbound.

decisions.consequence: Triggers silent alarm. Demos senses the Force disturbance. But gains a Jedi ally and saves a man from the dungeon.

decisions.choice: Attempt to eavesdrop on the Demos/Draco holo-call
decisions.consequence: Reveals Draco is inbound with the Assiduous. Death clock starts. Critical intelligence.

narrativeLinks.note: Rescuing Denia triggers the fortress alarm and Demos's interception in Scene 5.

skillChecks.context: Slipping into the restricted lower levels.
skillChecks.success: Clean entry. No guards notice.
skillChecks.failure: A guard sees movement. Must be dealt with quietly.

skillChecks.context: Disabling the stasis pod's life support to free Denia safely.
skillChecks.success: Clean disconnect. Denia wakes slowly, disoriented but alive.
skillChecks.failure: Alarm triggers. Igren Demos senses the Force disturbance. Lockdown imminent.

skillChecks.context: Bypassing the high-security cell locks.
skillChecks.success: Locks open silently. No alarm.
skillChecks.failure: Lock triggers backup alarm. Guards dispatched.

skillChecks.context: Stabilizing Denia after ~2 years in stasis.
skillChecks.success: Denia is weak but mobile. Can walk with support.
skillChecks.failure: Denia is barely conscious. Must be carried. All movement checks are stepped up.

skillChecks.context: Resisting Denia's telepathic plea — it's emotionally overwhelming.
skillChecks.success: You feel her pain but maintain composure. Clear decision-making.
skillChecks.failure: The emotional weight is staggering. [Rattled] until you act on it or leave.

skillChecks.context: Eavesdropping on Demos's holo-call with Inquisitor Draco.
skillChecks.success: Full conversation overheard. Draco: 'I am on my way, with the Assiduous.' Death clock confirmed.
skillChecks.failure: Partial audio. Know someone Imperial is coming but not the timeline.

encounters.name: Elite Guard Droids
encounters.trigger: If alarm triggers or droids detect intruders
encounters.description: Two elite security droids activate and engage. Heavy armor, integrated weapons.
encounters.tactics: Droids fight methodically. Ion weapons are extremely effective.

environmentMechanics.name: Stasis Machinery
environmentMechanics.effect: Disconnecting improperly kills Denia. Tech check required.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Careful technical work. Slicer Spike provides +1 tier on the check.

environmentMechanics.name: Force Echo
environmentMechanics.effect: Force-sensitive characters feel nausea and dread. Igren Demos senses the disturbance from anywhere in the palace.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Nothing. This is unavoidable if Denia is freed.

rewards.narrative: Jedi Master Denia rescued. Warrick Raden freed from the dungeons. Varth's first red flag revealed. Draco inbound.

pacing.openingBeat: The restricted lower levels. Cold, dark, humming machinery.
pacing.risingAction: Discovery of the stasis pod. Denia's telepathic plea. Raden in the adjacent cell.
pacing.climax: Varth's voice: 'Grab her.' Eavesdrop on Demos/Draco: 'I am on my way.' The alarm sounds.
pacing.resolution: The heroes have Denia and Raden. The fortress is going hostile. Run.

scenes.title: The Escape
scenes.subtitle: The Majordomo's Stand
scenes.readAloud:
Klaxons scream through the stone corridors. The fortress goes into lockdown — blast doors grinding shut, red emergency lighting flooding every hall.

Blocking your path stands Igren Demos, the Neimoidian Majordomo. He is no longer simpering. He holds a vibro-staff with lethal confidence, flanked by his elite Trandoshan bodyguards.

His red eyes widen as he senses the Jedi behind you. "The Jedi... she's awake. Give her to me. I will take her to the Inquisitorius myself and claim what Varga never could."
scenes.gmNotes:
Demos is the mini-boss of Adventure 2. He reveals his Force-sensitivity (untrained) and uses minor Force powers — Force Slam to separate heroes, Force Pull to yank objects or people. His elite Trandoshan bodyguards provide additional threat.

DEMOS'S MOTIVATION: He wants Denia for himself. He plans to turn her over to the Inquisitorius and buy his way into Imperial favor. He's deluding himself — the Empire is deeply speciesist and doesn't trust Neimoidians (associated with the Separatist cause). They would take Denia and discard Demos. He dies chasing a reward he'd never receive — which quietly mirrors what the heroes are doing by chasing Varth's payday.

PERSUASION OPTION: A Tier 2 Persuasion check can convince Demos to stand down by pointing out that Varga has already fled without him — Demos has been abandoned. If successful, Demos hesitates, realizes the truth, and steps aside in bitter rage. If failed, he fights to the death.

DENIA'S MOMENT: When Demos falls (or stands down), Denia senses his untrained Force sensitivity. 'He was touched by the Force... untrained, but there.' This humanizes the kill and establishes Denia's sensitivity.

Running battle through sealed corridors. Gamorreans and Trandoshans converge. Speed is more valuable than kills. Every round spent fighting is a round Varga gains toward escape.

npcs.name: Igren Demos
npcs.notes: Untrained Force-sensitive. Uses vibro-staff and minor Force powers. Wants to claim Denia for the Inquisitorius. (Recurring — same stat block as Scene 2.)

passive.name: Untrained Sensitivity
passive.description: Demos can sense Force disturbances but cannot control it. He senses Denia's awakening and any Force use in the palace.

actions.name: Force Slam
actions.description: Crude Force push. Targets Endure. Knocks target back 1 zone.

actions.name: Vibro-Staff Strike
actions.description: Melee attack with vibro-staff.

maneuver.name: Force Pull
maneuver.description: Yank one object or person 1 zone closer.

exploit.name: Desperate Surge
exploit.description: Once per encounter, double Power on a Force attack. Costs 2 Vitality.

threatBuild.notes: Elite mini-boss. Untrained Force user — dangerous but sloppy. Dies in this scene or stands down via Persuasion.

npcs.name: Trandoshan Bodyguards
npcs.notes: Demos's personal muscle. Same stats as Scene 1 Trandoshan Mercenaries.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore first [Weakened] per scene.

actions.name: Vibroblade Chop
actions.description: Heavy melee attack. +1 Power.

actions.name: Claw Rake
actions.description: Unarmed attack. Natural weapons.

maneuver.name: Shield Demos
maneuver.description: Interpose between Demos and attacker. Take the hit instead.

exploit.name: Regeneration
exploit.description: Heal 1 Vitality at end of turn.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Brutes. Bodyguard behavior — they protect Demos.

scenes.hazards: Force powers — Slam and Pull. Environmental hazards (braziers, statues). Narrow corridors favor Demos's staff. Fortress lockdown — blast doors sealing, hostile forces converging.

decisions.consequence: Tough fight but clears the path. Risk: Varga gains more escape time.

decisions.choice: Convince Demos that Varga has abandoned him
decisions.consequence: Persuasion T2 — if successful, Demos steps aside in bitter rage. Saves time and lives.

decisions.choice: Split the party — some fight, some run for the hangar
decisions.consequence: Risky but potentially catches Varga. Smaller groups are more vulnerable.

skillChecks.context: Resisting Demos's Force Slam.
skillChecks.success: Stand your ground. The Force push washes over you.
skillChecks.failure: Knocked back 1 zone. [Disoriented] for 1 round.

skillChecks.context: Reading Demos's Force abilities — is he trained?
skillChecks.success: Untrained. Powerful raw talent but no discipline. His attacks are predictable.
skillChecks.failure: Terrifying display. His abilities seem formidable.

skillChecks.context: Convincing Demos to stand down — Varga has already abandoned him.
skillChecks.success: Demos hesitates. Realizes Varga is fleeing without him. Steps aside in bitter rage.
skillChecks.failure: Demos is too far gone. Fights to the death.

skillChecks.context: Forcing through blast doors during fortress lockdown.
skillChecks.success: Door forced open. Path cleared.
skillChecks.failure: Door holds. Must find alternate route — costs time.

skillChecks.context: Hacking lockdown controls to open sealed corridors.
skillChecks.success: Blast doors retract. Clear path to the hangar wing.
skillChecks.failure: Partial override. Some doors open, others stay sealed.

encounters.name: Igren Demos Showdown
encounters.trigger: Heroes encounter Demos in the corridor
encounters.description: Demos with vibro-staff and minor Force powers, flanked by 2 Trandoshan bodyguards. Mini-boss fight.
encounters.tactics: Demos uses Force Slam to separate the party. Bodyguards flank isolated targets. Focus fire on the strongest-looking PC.

environmentMechanics.name: Narrow Corridor
environmentMechanics.effect: Only 2 characters can engage Demos simultaneously. Limits flanking.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use ranged attacks from behind the front line.

environmentMechanics.name: Force Hazards
environmentMechanics.effect: Braziers and statues become projectiles. Environmental damage in the zone.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Take cover behind pillars. Force projectiles are indirect.

environmentMechanics.name: Blast Door Lockdown
environmentMechanics.effect: Sealed durasteel doors block major corridors. Athletics or Tech to bypass.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Fusion Cutter cuts through in 1 action. Tech hack opens them remotely.

rewards.narrative: Demos is defeated or persuaded to stand down. Denia senses his Force sensitivity. Path to the hangar is clear.

pacing.openingBeat: Klaxons. Red light. Demos blocks the corridor. He is no longer simpering.
pacing.risingAction: Force powers and bodyguards. A real fight. Or a desperate argument.
pacing.climax: Demos is cornered — fight or talk?
pacing.resolution: Demos falls or surrenders. Denia's quiet observation. The hangar awaits.

scenes.title: The Hangar Bay
scenes.subtitle: Climax
scenes.readAloud:
You burst through the blast doors into the humid, open-air hangar. You're too late — Varga's massive luxury yacht, the Glorious Chariot, is already hovering on repulsors, engines flaring blue-white. The boarding ramp retracts as the gold-and-purple hull begins to rise.

But it's not just Varga. Lady Sinde has ordered her Stormtrooper escort to secure the hangar and eliminate all witnesses.

Blaster fire erupts from behind crates of Tibanna gas as white-armored figures take firing positions.

Warrick Raden stumbles onto the ship's ramp behind you, blinking in the harsh light. For the first time since the dungeon, he looks hopeful.

Then Admiral Varth steps forward. He looks at Raden. Almost warmly.

"Hello, Warrick." A pause. "I told you the next time I saw you, I would kill you."

The blaster shot echoes across the hangar. Raden drops. Varth doesn't break stride.
scenes.gmNotes:
This is the climax of Adventure 2 — three things happen simultaneously:

1. VARGA ESCAPES: The Glorious Chariot lifts off. Varga is fleeing to his fallback on Bespin (Cloud City). The heroes cannot stop him here — his escape is narratively required. He collapses part of the ceiling with the yacht's exhaust to discourage pursuit.

2. SINDE'S STORMTROOPERS: Lady Sinde has ordered her 4-Stormtrooper escort to eliminate all witnesses. She is protecting Project Leviathan's secrecy. The heroes must fight across the hangar using crates and equipment for cover. Tibanna gas crates are explosive if hit by blaster fire — area denial that works both ways.

3. VARTH EXECUTES RADEN: This is the second red flag — and far worse than the first. The heroes just rescued Raden from the dungeons. They've been protecting him since Scene 1. He's walking toward freedom on the ship's ramp. Varth greets him, then draws and shoots him between the eyes. 'I told you the next time I saw you, I would kill you.' The line references their last dealing — when Varth discovered Raden was running his own skim. It sounds personal. It's not. It's operational. Varth is eliminating a liability who knew too much about the embezzlement pipeline. Varth does not explain or apologize. He checks the body once and moves to the ship.

TRAJECTORY LOCK: If the heroes get to their ship fast enough, a Tactics check gets a partial trajectory lock on Varga's escape vector. The vector aligns with the Anoat Sector. Bespin. Cloud City. This connects to Gome's intel from Scene 3.

ENDING: The adventure ends on the ship, in hyperspace. Denia is weak and confused. Raden is dead. Varth is analyzing the trajectory data. The heroes have partial encryption keys (or not), a trajectory to Bespin, and two disturbing memories of the man they're working for.

npcs.name: Lady Sinde
npcs.notes: Ordering the elimination of all witnesses. (Recurring — same stat block as Scene 3.)

passive.name: Imperial Authority
passive.description: Lady Sinde commands Stormtroopers. Her orders are obeyed without question.

actions.name: Direct Fire
actions.description: Order a Stormtrooper to attack immediately.

actions.name: Cutting Assessment
actions.description: Social attack that dismantles cover stories. Targets Resist.

maneuver.name: Issue Orders
maneuver.description: One ally gains a bonus maneuver.

gambit.name: Ultimatum
gambit.description: Target must comply or be marked for elimination.

exploit.name: Call Reinforcements
exploit.description: Summon 4 Stormtrooper minions to her location.

threatBuild.notes: Commander. Resist 5. (Recurring — same stat block as Scene 3.)

npcs.name: Stormtrooper Squad
npcs.notes: Securing the hangar. Professional and lethal.

passive.name: Disciplined Fire
passive.description: +1 Power on weapon attacks.

actions.name: E-11 Volley
actions.description: Ranged attack.

actions.name: Bayonet Strike
actions.description: Melee attack.

maneuver.name: Take Cover
maneuver.description: Move to cover. Gain [Cover 1].

exploit.name: Coordinated Fire
exploit.description: All Troopers on same target gain +1 Power.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Stormtrooper minions. Same as Adv1. Power 3. Defense 4. Masterful+ = takedown.

scenes.hazards: Open hangar bay. Tibanna gas crates (explosive if hit). Collapsing ceiling from Varga's escape. Stormtrooper crossfire.

decisions.choice: Fight to the ship and pursue Varga
decisions.consequence: Can get a trajectory lock on Varga's escape vector. Leads to Cloud City.

decisions.choice: Eliminate Lady Sinde and the Stormtroopers
decisions.consequence: Removes Imperial witnesses but costs time. Varga gets further away.

narrativeLinks.note: Varga flees to Cloud City, setting up the entire Adventure 3 pursuit.

skillChecks.context: Firefight across the hangar bay.
skillChecks.success: Accurate fire. Stormtroopers suppressed or eliminated.
skillChecks.failure: Pinned by return fire. [Suppressed] for 1 round.

skillChecks.context: Hot-wiring the heroes' ship or bypassing hangar controls.
skillChecks.success: Ship systems online. Ready for launch.
skillChecks.failure: Delayed launch. 2 additional rounds under fire.

skillChecks.context: Launching under heavy fire with Stormtroopers targeting the ship.
skillChecks.success: Clean launch. Break atmosphere.
skillChecks.failure: Ship takes hits during liftoff. Hull damage. Emergency repairs needed.

skillChecks.context: Tracking Varga's escape trajectory.
skillChecks.success: Partial trajectory lock. The vector aligns with the Anoat Sector. Bespin. Cloud City.
skillChecks.failure: Lost him. Must rely on Gome's intel to find Varga.

skillChecks.context: Sprinting across the open hangar to the ship.
skillChecks.success: Full speed. Reach the ship.
skillChecks.failure: Stumble or take fire. Slowed.

encounters.name: Hangar Bay Firefight
encounters.description: Lady Sinde's Stormtroopers are eliminating witnesses. 8 troopers in firing positions behind crates.
encounters.tactics: Stormtroopers use Tibanna crates as cover. Shooting the crates causes explosions — area damage but clears positions.

environmentMechanics.name: Tibanna Gas Crates
environmentMechanics.effect: Explosive decompression. [Hazard 3] in the zone. Destroys cover.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't shoot near the crates. Or do — area denial works both ways.

environmentMechanics.name: Collapsing Ceiling
environmentMechanics.effect: Rubble blocks sections of the hangar. Difficult terrain. Some paths completely blocked.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Athletics to climb over. Tech to find alternate routes.

environmentMechanics.name: Open Air Hangar
environmentMechanics.effect: Takodana jungle visible below. Falls from the platform are survivable but result in a 2-scene delay.

rewards.narrative: Escape from Varga's fortress. Trajectory to Bespin/Cloud City. Denia rescued. Raden executed by Varth. The adventure ends on the ship, in hyperspace, with two red flags and a dead man's memory.

pacing.openingBeat: Burst through blast doors into the hangar. Varga's yacht is already lifting off.
pacing.risingAction: Stormtroopers open fire. Crates explode. Ceiling collapses.
pacing.climax: Varth executes Raden on the ramp. The sprint to the ship under fire.
pacing.resolution: Liftoff. Hyperspace. Silence. Varth's trajectory lock on Bespin.
